mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 2000 units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month decline in the federal funds rate below 4% signals a deepening monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month increase in industrial production above 110 signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 11500 signals a strengthening economy that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening economy periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
